macro_segments.title: Introduction and Overview of Redemption Laws for Sold Fields

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and daf identification
Mishna: Lo yimkor berachok - prohibition on selling distant fields to redeem nearby ones

macro_segments.title: Gemara Analysis of Matza (Finding Money) and the Hermeneutic Debate

micro_segments.title: Mena hanei milei - sources for the limitations on redemption
Detanu rabanan - the rabbis taught: conditions on redemption including yad atzmo, shelo yilaveh, umatza
Gemara's hermeneutic debate on the meaning of matza in different contexts
Rabbi Eliezer's case of the stone flying from the hand
Rava's resolution: contextual interpretation based on pesach themes

macro_segments.title: Comparison of Redemption Rights for Sold Fields vs. Consecrated Fields

micro_segments.title: Hekdesh case: ואם גאל יגאל - broader rights for redeeming consecrated fields
Rabbi Shimon's logical explanation: mah ta'am - rationale for different rules
Tannaitic debate: two baraitot on borrowing and partial redemption
Tosafot's analysis: principle application to batei arei chomah

macro_segments.title: Batei Arei Chomah (Walled City Houses): Basic Rules and Redemption

micro_segments.title: Hamocher bayit - sale of house in walled city with one-year redemption right
Harei zeh kemin ribbit oveino ribbit - conceptual status as quasi-interest
Met hamocher yigal beno and met halokeach yigal beno - succession rights
Ein monin shanah - year measured from date of sale, not calendar year
Rebbi's minority opinion: solar year instead of lunar year

macro_segments.title: Gemara on Year Measurement: From Date vs. Calendar

micro_segments.title: Matnitin d'lo k'Rabbi - our mishna does not follow Rabbi's view
Yamim - two-day waiting period according to Rabbi
Mi-yom l'yom me'et l'et - measuring to the exact moment, not just the day
Digression on Bar Mitzvah and Bechor: application of year-measurement principles

macro_segments.title: Interest (Ribbit) Analysis in the Context of Conditional Sale

micro_segments.title: Harei zeh ribbit gemura - tannaitic debate on nature of interest
Rivka case: field sold with right to repurchase and buyer eating fruits
Rav Papa's analysis: one-sided ribbit and intent to return
Rashi's distinction: hekdesh case vs. conditional sale framing

macro_segments.title: Multiple Sales and Succession of Rights in Walled City Houses

micro_segments.title: Rabbanan: year measured from original sale to first buyer
Lemi chalut: debate whether first or second buyer retains permanent title after year
Rabbi Abba bar Memel: kol zchus shetavo l'yado - all rights transfer to subsequent buyer

macro_segments.title: Calendar Issues: Leap Years and Adar I/II

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Abba case: two houses sold in Adar I vs. Adar II, year completion dates differ
Ravina's challenge: fairness argument about grazing time before one's year ends
Lambs case: bechor and animal age measured by calendar date not day count
Gezeira shava: shana shana mehadadi gamri - year measured by same calendar date
Rebbi's solar year position: 365 days vs. rabbis' lunar 12-month approach

macro_segments.title: Gift (Matana) in Walled City Houses and Perpetual Ownership

micro_segments.title: Higia yom shneim asar chodesh - twelve months, no redemption, complete transfer
L'tzmitut - for perpetuity, including gift cases
Derasha: l'tzmitut l'rabbot et hamatana - gifts included via l'tzmitut
Debate with Rabbi Meir: matana eina k'mecher - gift not like sale for yovel
Rav Pappa resolution: l'tzmitut overrides Rabbi Meir's position on gifts

macro_segments.title: Consecrated Houses in Walled Cities and Yovel

micro_segments.title: Hamakdish bayit b'batei arei chomah - redemption of consecrated houses
Goalo acher miyad hekdesh - when third party purchases from hekdesh
L'dorosav - perpetual ownership excludes hekdesh (no generations)
Lo yeitzei bayovel - houses in walled cities do not return in yovel

Met hamocher yigal beno (seller's son redeems)
Met halokeach yigal beno (buyer's son's liability)
Kol zchus shetavo l'yado (all rights transfer)
Matana eina k'mecher (gift not like sale)
Rabbi Meir opinion on gifts and yovel
